Laborers drag limestone blocks up earthen ramps using palm-fiber ropes and wooden rollers beneath.
Merchants argue over copper ingots while scribes mark clay tablets recording the transaction weights.
Women pound grain with wooden pestles in tall mortars, creating flour for the day's bread.
Potters kick-wheel clay vessels while apprentices carry leather-hard jars to drying racks in sun.
Reed boat crews pole cargo upstream, their papyrus bundles stacked high with amphorae lashed tight.
Priests carry bronze censers trailing frankincense smoke toward an altar stacked with fruit offerings.
Bronze-smiths hammer glowing metal on stone anvils while bellows-workers pump air through clay nozzles.
Children chase sacred ibis birds between date palms while women balance water jars homeward.
Soldiers drill with leather shields and bronze-tipped spears in precise formation under a commander's shouts.
Weavers work vertical looms with linen thread while carders prepare flax fibers in woven baskets.
Farmers guide wooden plows behind oxen through irrigation-darkened fields, planting emmer wheat in furrows.
Washerwomen beat linen against river stones while water carriers ascend steps with yoked buckets.
Grain threshers beat emmer wheat with flails while boys drive oxen over harvested stalks.
Fishermen haul nets from wooden boats while others gut catch and pack them in salt.
Stonemasons chisel hieroglyphs into limestone blocks using copper chisels and wooden mallets precisely.
Bead-makers drill carnelian stones with bow-drills while apprentices polish finished pieces with sand.
Donkey caravans unload cedar logs while merchants inspect timber quality and haggle over prices.
Temple sweepers clear courtyard sand while incense bearers prepare myrrh for the evening ritual.
Women spin flax into thread on drop spindles while sitting in shade near doorways.
Brick-makers pack mud into wooden molds while others stack sun-dried bricks into tall rows.
Scribes squat with papyrus scrolls recording grain measurements as porters empty woven storage baskets.
Chariot teams race across packed earth while grooms wait with water and replacement wheels.
Dyers stir indigo vats with sticks while assistants wring dyed linen and hang dripping cloth.
Farmers clear irrigation channels with bronze hoes while boys redirect water flow using mud dams.
Metalworkers pour molten bronze from clay crucibles into stone molds shaped for axe heads.
